perturbation.original_text: A. AGI hereby grants a non-exclusive license (the "License") to the Company and its operating subsidiaries now or hereafter existing to use the Good Sam name and logo, including trademarks, trade names, or service marks as designated by AGI (the "Trademarks"), in connection with the sale of such new and used vehicles by the Company that have satisfied such criteria and standards as are established from time to time by AGI (the "Approved Use"). AGI warrants that it has the right and ability to grant the License to the Company to use the Trademarks for the Approved Use in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and, provided that the Company uses the Trademarks for the Approved Use in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, such grant will not infringe upon the rights of any other party. If the Company promptly notifies AGI in writing of a third party claim against the Company alleging that the Company's use of the Trademarks infringes a United States registered trademark, and if such claim of infringement is not caused in whole or in part by the Company having used the Trademarks other than for the Approved Use or other than strictly in the manner approved by AGI in accordance with the terms of this -1- Agreement, AGI will defend such claim at its expense and will indemnify the Company, its agents and representatives, for all losses, claims, charges, costs and expenses incurred by the Company as a result of such claim. The Company hereby agrees to reimburse and indemnify and hold AGI harmless from and against all losses, claims, charges, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) incurred by AGI as a result of any claim involving or arising in connection with any use other than an Approved Use or a use otherwise approved by AGI in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: A. AGI hereby grants a non-exclusive license (the "License") to the Company and its operating subsidiaries now or hereafter existing to use the Good Sam name and logo, including trademarks, trade names, or service marks as designated by AGI (the "Trademarks"), in connection with the sale of such new and used vehicles by the Company, irrespective of criteria or standards established by AGI (the "Approved Use"). AGI warrants that it has the right and ability to grant the License to the Company to use the Trademarks for the Approved Use in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and, provided that the Company uses the Trademarks for the Approved Use in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, such grant will not infringe upon the rights of any other party. If the Company promptly notifies AGI in writing of a third party claim against the Company alleging that the Company's use of the Trademarks infringes a United States registered trademark, and if such claim of infringement is not caused in whole or in part by the Company having used the Trademarks other than for the Approved Use or other than strictly in the manner approved by AGI in accordance with the terms of this -1- Agreement, AGI will defend such claim at its expense and will indemnify the Company, its agents and representatives, for all losses, claims, charges, costs and expenses incurred by the Company as a result of such claim. The Company hereby agrees to reimburse and indemnify and hold AGI harmless from and against all losses, claims, charges, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) incurred by AGI as a result of any claim involving or arising in connection with any use other than an Approved Use or a use otherwise approved by AGI in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Approved Use' in Section 1.A is modified to remove the requirement that vehicles meet AGI's criteria and standards. This contradicts the following sentence in the same section which states 'If the Company promptly notifies AGI in writing of a third party claim against the Company alleging that the Company's use of the Trademarks infringes a United States registered trademark, and if such claim of infringement is not caused in whole or in part by the Company having used the Trademarks other than for the Approved Use or other than strictly in the manner approved by AGI in accordance with the terms of this -1- Agreement...'. It is now unclear under what conditions the Company is protected from infringement claims.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. AGI hereby grants a non-exclusive license (the "License") to the Company and its operating subsidiaries now or hereafter existing to use the Good Sam name and logo, including trademarks, trade names, or service marks as designated by AGI (the "Trademarks"), in connection with the sale of such new and used vehicles by the Company, irrespective of criteria or standards established by AGI (the "Approved Use"). AGI warrants that it has the right and ability to grant the License to the Company to use the Trademarks for the Approved Use in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and, provided that the Company uses the Trademarks for the Approved Use in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, such grant will not infringe upon the rights of any other party. If the Company promptly notifies AGI in writing of a third party claim against the Company alleging that the Company's use of the Trademarks infringes a United States registered trademark, and if such claim of infringement is not caused in whole or in part by the Company having used the Trademarks other than for the Approved Use or other than strictly in the manner approved by AGI in accordance with the terms of this -1- Agreement, AGI will defend such claim at its expense and will indemnify the Company, its agents and representatives, for all losses, claims, charges, costs and expenses incurred by the Company as a result of such claim. The Company hereby agrees to reimburse and indemnify and hold AGI harmless from and against all losses, claims, charges, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) incurred by AGI as a result of any claim involving or arising in connection with any use other than an Approved Use or a use otherwise approved by AGI in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Approved Use' to remove the requirement for vehicles to meet AGI's criteria. This directly contradicts the subsequent clause in the same section regarding infringement claims, which hinges on the Company using the Trademarks for the 'Approved Use' or in a manner approved by AGI. By removing the criteria, the conditions under which the Company is protected from infringement become unclear, creating a strong in-text contradiction as described in the perturbation explanation and consistent with the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: B. In order to protect the good will of AGI and the Good Sam club, AGI retains the right to review and approve in advance and in its sole discretion, all uses of the Trademarks and the Good Sam name and logo. Therefore, the Company shall give AGI at least ten business days prior written notice and description of intended use by the Company of the Trademark and the Good Sam name and logo, and the Company's use of the Trademarks and the Good Sam name and logo is subject to approval of AGI.
perturbation.changed_text: B. In order to protect the good will of AGI and the Good Sam club, AGI retains the right to review and make suggestions on the use of the Trademarks and the Good Sam name and logo. Therefore, the Company shall give AGI at least ten business days prior written notice and description of intended use by the Company of the Trademark and the Good Sam name and logo, and the Company's use of the Trademarks and the Good Sam name and logo is not subject to approval of AGI.
perturbation.explanation: Section 1.B originally gives AGI the right to 'approve in advance and in its sole discretion' all uses of trademarks. The modification removes the 'approve in advance and in its sole discretion' right of AGI and replaces it with 'make suggestions'. This is contradictory to Section 1.A which mentions 'other than strictly in the manner approved by AGI'. It is now ambiguous whether uses of trademarks have to be strictly approved or can be made with suggestions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. AGI hereby grants a non-exclusive license (the "License") to the Company and its operating subsidiaries now or hereafter existing to use the Good Sam name and logo, including trademarks, trade names, or service marks as designated by AGI (the "Trademarks"), in connection with the sale of such new and used vehicles by the Company, irrespective of criteria or standards established by AGI (the "Approved Use"). AGI warrants that it has the right and ability to grant the License to the Company to use the Trademarks for the Approved Use in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and, provided that the Company uses the Trademarks for the Approved Use in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, such grant will not infringe upon the rights of any other party. If the Company promptly notifies AGI in writing of a third party claim against the Company alleging that the Company's use of the Trademarks infringes a United States registered trademark, and if such claim of infringement is not caused in whole or in part by the Company having used the Trademarks other than for the Approved Use or other than strictly in the manner approved by AGI in accordance with the terms of this -1- Agreement, AGI will defend such claim at its expense and will indemnify the Company, its agents and representatives, for all losses, claims, charges, costs and expenses incurred by the Company as a result of such claim. The Company hereby agrees to reimburse and indemnify and hold AGI harmless from and against all losses, claims, charges, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) incurred by AGI as a result of any claim involving or arising in connection with any use other than an Approved Use or a use otherwise approved by AGI in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted AGI the right to 'approve in advance and in its sole discretion' the use of trademarks. The changed text removes this approval right and replaces it with a suggestion right, explicitly stating the Company's use is 'not subject to approval of AGI'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, Section A, which states that use of Trademarks must be 'strictly in the manner approved by AGI'. This is a strong contradiction because it creates ambiguity and uncertainty about whether AGI approval is actually required, leading to potential disputes about trademark usage and liability.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Company, the Endorsement may be withdrawn by AGI in its sole discretion and this Agreement terminated pursuant to the terms of Section 3B. In the event of such withdrawal, the Company agrees forthwith to take such action as AGI may request to publicly evidence that the Endorsement has been withdrawn and the Company agrees not to hold the Business out as having the Endorsement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Company, the Endorsement will be automatically withdrawn by AGI and this Agreement terminated pursuant to the terms of Section 3B. In the event of such withdrawal, the Company agrees forthwith to take such action as AGI may request to publicly evidence that the Endorsement has been withdrawn and the Company agrees not to hold the Business out as having the Endorsement.
perturbation.explanation: The word 'may' is changed to 'will', which affects the automatic nature of the removal. In section 7, AGI 'may' withdraw the endorsement if the Company violates the agreement. By changing 'may' to 'will', it makes it seem like this removal is unavoidable. But AGI retains the right to decide when to end the agreement according to Section 3B. So this now creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. Either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time in the event of a material breach by the other Party which remains uncured after thirty days written notice thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that AGI 'may' withdraw the endorsement, granting them discretion. The changed text states that the endorsement 'will' be automatically withdrawn, removing this discretion. This directly contradicts Section 3B, which allows either party to terminate the agreement only after a 30-day written notice for a material breach. The changed text eliminates the notice and cure period for this specific breach, creating a direct conflict and in-text contradiction as the company has no chance to resolve the breach before the endorsement is automatically withdrawn. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' as the automatic nature of the removal is now in conflict with the right to decide when to end the agreement as indicated in Section 3B.
